wiki.summary: "House Tour" is a song by American singer Sabrina Carpenter and the fourth single from her seventh studio album "Man's Best Friend" (2025). It was written by Carpenter alongside Amy Allen, Jack Antonoff, and John Ryan, and produced by Carpenter, Antonoff, and Ryan. Musically, the track has been described as bubblegum pop, dance-pop, power pop, and synth-pop, incorporating elements of post-disco and 1980s-inspired production. The song’s style has drawn comparisons to artists such as Janet Jackson and Paula Abdul. Read more on Last.fm.
wiki.content: "House Tour" is a song by American singer Sabrina Carpenter and the fourth single from her seventh studio album "Man's Best Friend" (2025). It was written by Carpenter alongside Amy Allen, Jack Antonoff, and John Ryan, and produced by Carpenter, Antonoff, and Ryan. Musically, the track has been described as bubblegum pop, dance-pop, power pop, and synth-pop, incorporating elements of post-disco and 1980s-inspired production. The song’s style has drawn comparisons to artists such as Janet Jackson and Paula Abdul. Lyrically, "House Tour" uses extended innuendos centered on the concept of inviting a romantic partner to the singer’s home, with sexual subtext present throughout the lyrics. The song includes references such as the cookie brand Chips Ahoy!, and features lines that directly acknowledge its suggestive language, including the lyric "and I promise none of this is a metaphor". "House Tour" was released as part of "Man's Best Friend" and later issued as a single. It received generally positive reviews from music critics. Some reviewers highlighted its production and playful lyrical style, while others noted its use of humor and innuendo. In a ranking of songs from the album, Jason Lipshutz placed it at number two, describing it as a fast-paced synth-pop track built around hooks and wordplay. Another critic, India Block, commented on the contrast between its domestic imagery and its underlying sexual themes. A 7-inch vinyl release of the song, featuring a remix titled "Back Door" by Fcukers, was scheduled for release in June 2026. A music video for "House Tour" was released on April 6, 2026. It was co-directed by Sabrina Carpenter and Margaret Qualley and shot in VistaVision. The video depicts Carpenter, Qualley, and Madelyn Cline as a group of thieves breaking into a mansion and stealing various items. The visual style and narrative drew comparisons to the film "Drive-Away Dolls", which also starred Qualley. The song was included on the setlist of Carpenter’s "Short n' Sweet Tour" (2024–2025) and was performed at several music festivals in 2026, including Lollapalooza events in Chile, Argentina, and Brazil, Asunciónico in Paraguay, and the Festival Estéreo Picnic in Bogotá. It was also used as the opening song for her headlining performance at Coachella 2026. Read more on Last.fm.
